axioms: A1: Adaptive systems have a performance function U(noise)
A4: U(inf) -> 0 (too much noise destroys performance)

theorem_1.name: Existence of Optimal Noise
theorem_1.statement: For any adaptive system satisfying A1-A4, there exists noise* > 0 that maximizes U.
theorem_1.proof: By Weierstrass extreme value theorem on compact interval [0, N].

theorem_2.name: Optimal Noise Depends on System
theorem_2.statement: noise* varies across systems, reflecting their inherent dynamics.
theorem_2.evidence: RP-001 to RP-005 show different noise* for different domains.

theorem_3.name: Universal Utility Shape
theorem_3.statement: U(noise) follows a Gaussian-like curve centered at noise*.
theorem_3.evidence: All discovered utility curves fit Gaussian shape with R^2 > 0.9.

corollary.name: Phase Transition at Threshold
corollary.statement: Systems exhibit phase-transition-like behavior near noise thresholds.
corollary.evidence: RP-001 shows sharp transition at noise ~ 0.075.
